message:
opfs-cached onnx fetch + warmup timing breakdown — beat tjs cold start

fetchBytesCached in src/js/model/opfs-fetch.ts wraps fetch with OPFS
storage; the ~770 MB model_q4f16.onnx_data blob is too big for Cache
API (quota exceeded on second entry) but fits comfortably in OPFS
where Chromium advertises ~60% of free disk and Safari 1 GB. Both
backends consume the same helper so a user who switches wasm↔webgpu
doesn't re-download.

Also overlaps pipeline compile with weight upload in warmup, saving
~600 ms that was previously serialized.

WebGpuBackend exposes warmupTimings so the browser test harness can
isolate adapter/device/fetch/parse/upload/compile costs independently.

Steady-state M-series/Chromium:

  first-visit warmup (fresh download):
    tjs=13.3s  wasm=14.1s  gpu=1.6s (OPFS hit via wasm's download)
  second-visit warmup (all caches warm):
    tjs=11.2s  wasm=1.8s   gpu=1.1s     (10x faster than tjs)
  per-forward (short/med/long):
    gpu=9.4/12.4/25.1 ms vs tjs=38.3/39.1/56.6 ms  (2.3–4x faster)

Parity unchanged: forward argmax 32/32, rms=0.18; matmul_int4
maxAbs=1.23e-3; span smoke 2/2 agreed vs tjs.
